Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: AssistedBundleBill
DataEntity.Documentation: Describe a bill. A bill is composed by multiple BillUsagePeriods. A bill's total charge is the summ of the sub-totals of all its BillUagesPeriods.

DataEntity.Name: AsstBundleBillDetail
DataEntity.Documentation: Describe a bill. A bill is composed by multiple BillUsagePeriods. A bill's total charge is the summ of the sub-totals of all its BillUagesPeriods.

DataEntity.Name: AsstBundleCompUsage
DataEntity.Documentation: Describe a company. A company can have multiple bills and usage periods.

DataEntity.Name: Bill
DataEntity.Documentation: Describe a bill. A bill is composed by multiple BillUsagePeriods. A bill's total charge is the summ of the sub-totals of all its BillUagesPeriods.

DataEntity.Name: BillingDetail
DataEntity.Documentation: A BillingDetail entity represents everything about one type of charge (i.e. SKU) for one bill.

Property.Name: ItemTotal
Property.Documentation: The total amount of this charge, including discount and sales-tax. This is the amount included in the offload total. Note that it may be 1 or 2 cents greater or less than the sum of (Quantity * UnitPrice - DiscountAmount + TaxAmount) due to rounding.

Property.Name: OffloadDate
Property.Documentation: When the bill is saved, this will be the date the offload is expected to happen. If the Sales Tax Exception batch process has updated this BillingDetail entitiy, this will be the date the offload actually happened.

Property.Name: TaxAmountWhenOffloaded
Property.Documentation: The sales tax amount allocated to this charge in effect at the time this charge was offloaded. It will be null prior to offload. It will be assigned shortly after offload by the Tax Exception batch is created. It will remain null if the Company is tax-exempt.

Property.Name: ServiceDate
Property.Documentation: The date when the billable service was fulfilled.

Property.Name: ItemName
Property.Documentation: The name of the OfferingServiceChargeGroup containing the OfferingServiceCharge for this entity's ItemSku.

Property.Name: ItemSku
Property.Documentation: The SKU being charged for.

Property.Name: Quantity
Property.Documentation: The number of units to charge for.

Property.Name: UnitPrice
Property.Documentation: The per-unit price for the OfferingServiceCharge, in effect on the ServiceDate.

Property.Name: OfferCd
Property.Documentation: The offer-code of the Offer applied to this charge. Will be null when no Offer applies.

Property.Name: OfferName
Property.Documentation: The name of the Offer applied to this charge. Will be null when no Offer applies.

Property.Name: DiscountAmount
Property.Documentation: The (positive) dollar discount amount of the Offer applied to this charge. When the Offer's DiscountType is PercentOff, this amount is (UnitPrice * Quantity * offer.DiscountPercent / 100), rounded to the nearest $0.01. The DiscountAmount will never exceed (UnitPrice * Quantity).

Property.Name: TaxAmount
Property.Documentation: The sales-tax amount allocated to this charge. It will represent the tax as of the date the BillingDetail was saved. This property will be null when the Company being billed is tax-exempt. If the company is not tax-exempt, this property will be the tax amount, or $0.00 when the specific SKU is not taxable in the relevant jurisdiction.

Property.Name: TaxComputedDate
Property.Documentation: The date when the TaxAmount was last computed. This will be the date when the charge was first added to the bill, or when the charge was last updated.

Property.Name: OfferingServiceChargeType
Property.Documentation: The OfferingServiceChargeType of the OfferingServiceChargeGroup containing the OfferingServiceCharge for this entity's ItemSku.

Property.Name: TaxJurisdiction
Property.Documentation: The name of the Offer applied to this charge. Will be null when no Offer applies.

Property.Name: TaxExceptionInd
Property.Documentation: When true, this BillingDetail should be included in processing for the Sales Tax Exception report, regardless of other inclusion criteria.

Property.Name: BillingPeriod
Property.Documentation: The billing period for the fee (i.e. month/year for MonthlyFee, etc.)

Property.Name: Memo
Property.Documentation: The value of the Memo field on the Liability Check.

Property.Name: BasePrice
Property.Documentation: The base price for the OfferingServiceCharge, in effect on the ServiceDate.

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: EventDetailSubtype
Property.Documentation: When EventDetailTypeCd is GenericEventDetail, this property tells how to interpret the Value

Property.Name: BeginDate
Property.Documentation: Date the Company started benefitting from the offer

Property.Name: EndDate
Property.Documentation: Valid only when the Offer's EndEvent is DateEvent or DurationEvent (and the BeginDate is established)

Property.Name: UsagesRemaining
Property.Documentation: Valid only when the Offer's EndEvent is PayrollUsageEvent

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: CompanyUsage
DataEntity.Documentation: Describe a company. A company can have multiple bills and usage periods.

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: EmployeeUsage
DataEntity.Documentation: Describe the paycheck usage associated with a particular employee in the BillUsagePeriod. It contains multiple paycheck activities during the period.

DataEntity.Name: Entitlement
DataEntity.Documentation: Represents a customer entitlement in the siebel system

Property.Name: EffectiveDate
Property.Documentation: This mapping from subtype to offering is valid starting on this date

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: LiabilityAdjustment
DataEntity.InternalNotes: ForeignKeyOrder(CompanyAdjustmentSubmission, Law, Employee, PayrollRun, QbdtTransactionInfo, CompanyLaw, Company)

Property.Name: EndDate
Property.Documentation: Valid only when EndEvent is DateEvent

Property.Name: DurationDays
Property.Documentation: Valid only when EndEvent is DurationEvent

Property.Name: UsagesAllowed
Property.Documentation: Valid only when EndEvent is PayrollUsageEvent

Property.Name: EffectiveDate
Property.Documentation: The earliest date when this offer can be applied to a charge

Property.Name: SKU
Property.Documentation: Must be unique across Offerings and [Tiered]OfferingServiceCharges

Property.Name: TierNumber
Property.Documentation: SKU must be unique across [Tiered]OfferingServiceCharges and Offerings

Property.Name: TierUnits
Property.Documentation: SKU must be unique across [Tiered]OfferingServiceCharges and Offerings

Property.Name: SKU
Property.Documentation: SKU must be unique across [Tiered]OfferingServiceCharges and Offerings

Property.Name: IsTier
Property.Documentation: SKU must be unique across [Tiered]OfferingServiceCharges and Offerings

DataEntity.Name: PaycheckUsage
DataEntity.Documentation:
Describe one paycheck activity. The paycheck status is either "created" or "voided". The BRMStatus indicates whether this paycheck activity is sent to BRM or not. The batch job employs the following steps to feed BRM:
1. Scan PaycheckUsage for all the unBRM'ed paychecks 2. Trace down to UsagePeriod and collect all the periods which need resynch (i.e. which contain any paycheck in step 1) 3. For every period in step 2, trace down and find out the period usage only for those already-BRM'ed paychecks. This is the usage we already billed 4. For every period in step 2, trace down and find out the period usage for all the paychecks, both BRM'ed and unBRM'ed. This is the usage we should bill. 5. Diff step 5 and 4 and send to BRM 6. If succuss, update all the unBRM'ed paychecks to BRM'ed

DataEntity.Name: PaycheckUsageHist
DataEntity.Documentation:
Additional information regarding duplicate paycheckUsages
1. AlreadyBilled - If the Bill is closed then we mark the new paycheckUsages as AlreadyBilled 2. UsageTransferred - If the Bill is open then we transfer the usage count from older employeeUsage to the new one.

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: UsagePeriod
DataEntity.Documentation:
Describe a usage period. A usagePeriod is the dynamic view of payroll usage for a company, i.e. contains the up-to-date information of how many paychecks the company issued and/or voided in this particular usage period.
Please don't confuse usage period with the billing period. They may share the same start date and end date, but they are two different concepts. One usage period can affect multiple bills due to paycheck void or back-dates.

DataEntity.Name: Address
DataEntity.Documentation: This table stores the various ADDRESSes of different entities like employee and company.
DataEntity.InternalNotes: AuditProperties(AddressLine1, AddressLine2, AddressLine3, City, Country, State, ZipCode, ZipCodeExtension)

DataEntity.Name: AuthUser
DataEntity.Documentation: This is going to be a temporary table, until we have requirements for REP PORTAL. The Primary reson to have this table is to store CORP_ID from Ldap user. We won't be storing password as authentication would be doen from LDAP. Assumption - Currently there is no way to maintain this table in ver1.0 for DD. For small set of finance users we would manually (from SQL) insert data for CORP_ID

DataEntity.Name: BankAccount
DataEntity.Documentation: Bank accounts associated with a company that typically are used to draft funds for payroll fees and employee checks.Transit number was removed July 2006 as it was deemed an unnecessary attribute for bank account. Here is the definition for all eternity ...Depository financial institution transit identification number assigned by the Federal Reserve Bank. This identifies the institution that handles the ACH process for the designated bank.

Property.Name: ACHAccountTypeCd
Property.Documentation: Type of bank acount that is being utilized - savings or checking.;

Property.Name: AccountTypeCd
Property.Documentation: Type of bank acount that is being utilized - savings or checking.;

Property.Name: RoutingNumber
Property.Documentation: Depository financial institution identification number assigned by the Federal Reserve Bank.

Property.Name: AccountNumberEnc
Property.Documentation: Number identifying the individual's account at the Depository Financial institution identified in Routing number.

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: Number identifying the individual's account at the Depository Financial institution identified in Routing number.

DataEntity.Name: Company
DataEntity.Documentation: This table contains an organization that uses one or more PSE services.

Property.Name: LegalName
Property.Documentation: Legal name of the company.

Property.Name: SourceCompanyId
Property.Documentation: Identification number in the source system.

Property.Name: NameControl
Property.Documentation: A name control is a sequence of characters derived from a taxpayer’s name that is used by IRS in processing the tax return filed by the taxpayer.

Property.Name: IAMRealmId
Property.Documentation: Legal name of the company.

Property.Name: FedTaxIdEnc
Property.Documentation: EIN (FEIN) - Unique in the DD service. Some prefixes are invalid which are yet to be defined by the requirements team.

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: CompanyBankAccount
DataEntity.Documentation: Association between company and bank.

Property.Name: SourceBankAccountId
Property.Documentation: A unique bank account identifier in the source system.

Property.Name: StatusCd
Property.Documentation: Bank account status - active, inactive, on-hold, etc.

Property.Name: SourceBankAccountName
Property.Documentation: The nickname the source system has given to this bank account

DataEntity.Name: CompanyNote
DataEntity.Documentation: This table will capture Notes associated with Company. Z_ins_dttm will be mapped for this table from toplink because its only for display purpose. User specific columns were removed.

DataEntity.Name: CompanyService
DataEntity.Documentation: The list of products and/or services a company has subscribed to.

DataEntity.Name: Contact
DataEntity.Documentation: Contact Person for a company. This table will store Primary contact and all other contacts for company. Currently for DD we assume the contact_type is always primary. We can add VAL table if need in other projects.
DataEntity.InternalNotes: AuditProperties(AuthSignerYnInd, ContactRoleCd, Fax, JobTitle, SourceContactId, Title)

Property.Name: ContactRoleCd
Property.Documentation: right now there is only one role. This could change in the future, but then that would simply use a val table.

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: DDCompanyServiceInfo
DataEntity.Documentation: A company that subscribes to Intuitl direct deposit service.

Property.Name: HighAnnualPayAmount
Property.Documentation: what is this?

DataEntity.Name: Employee
DataEntity.Documentation: This table stores information pertaining to a particular employee of a company. For example, hire date, termination date etc.

Property.Name: TaxIdEnc
Property.Documentation: commonly referred to as the social security number

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: EmployeeBankAccount
DataEntity.Documentation: The financial institution that an employee feels safe to conduct financial matters with. For example they could have a savings account to save money for retirement, or a checking account to write checks to pay bills.

Property.Name: SourceBankAccountId
Property.Documentation: A unique bank account identifier in the source system.

Property.Name: StatusCd
Property.Documentation: Bank account status - active, inactive, on-hold, etc.

Property.Name: SourceBankAccountIdEnc
Property.Documentation: A unique bank account identifier in the source system.

DataEntity.Name: Fee
DataEntity.Documentation: Money we charge the client for services rendered by PSE

DataEntity.Name: FinancialTransaction
DataEntity.Documentation: The Entity will store Financial Transcations associated with a Company. These transaction could be DD for employee, Third Party Payments for Vendor, Tax Liabilities, Employee Benefit liab, Employer txn. The implicit transactions would be determined by BO, there won't be any posting rules for financial txn at Database level. If we realize that all these type of txns have different attribute then we can have sub-type entities. For now we can see all of the different types of txns have similar attributes

Property.Name: FinancialTransactionAmount
Property.Documentation: Amount of the transaction.

Property.Name: SettlementDate
Property.Documentation: Date that the transaction is processed. No back dating allowed, but future processing is anticipated.

Property.Name: Sku
Property.Documentation: For transactions representing fees or sales-tax on fees, this is the fee SKU. For others, this is null.

Property.Name: OriginalSettlementDate
Property.Documentation: Date that the transaction is processed. No back dating allowed, but future processing is anticipated.

DataEntity.Name: FinancialTransactionState
DataEntity.Documentation: The particular status of a transaction at a specific momemnt in time.

DataEntity.Name: Individual
DataEntity.Documentation: A general class representing a person - a specific instance of a human being.

DataEntity.Name: IntuitBankAccount
DataEntity.Documentation: This table contains Intuit bank accounts used for such things as fees, taxes, payroll, etc.

DataEntity.Name: IntuitBankAccountTransactionType
DataEntity.Documentation: This table contains a pairing of an Intuit account to a transaction type. There are certain transactions - e.g. writeoff transfer - that have two Intuit bank accounts.

Property.Name: DepositFrequencyFk
Property.Documentation: Obsolete property - do not use

Property.Name: AgencyTaxpayerIdEnc
Property.Documentation: For payments, this is the value of CompanyAgency.AgencyTaxpayerId at the time the MMT was created

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: OffloadBatch
DataEntity.Documentation: Batch of transaction sent to MME. This table will be used and populated by Txn Executor.

Property.Name: StatusCd
Property.Documentation: Status of a offload Batch. Not Null field default value would be InProcess when its created and Completed when it is completed

DataEntity.Name: OffloadGroup
DataEntity.Documentation: This entity will store offload frequency that will calculate cuttoff time. Each company will be associated with one offload frequency.Gray as of 4/10 per Steve K.

DataEntity.Name: Paycheck
DataEntity.Documentation: A exchange of money for services between a company and employee, respectively.

Property.Name: NetAmount
Property.Documentation: Total amount of the entire paycheck.

Property.Name: GrossAmount
Property.Documentation: Total amount of the entire paycheck.

Property.Name: YTDGrossAmount
Property.Documentation: Total amount of the entire paycheck.

Property.Name: YTDNetAmount
Property.Documentation: Total amount of the entire paycheck.

DataEntity.Name: PaycheckSplit
DataEntity.Documentation: This table will contain how a paycheck is dispursed into several account. For example, a paycheck can go 50% into checking and 50% into savings.

Property.Name: SourceDdTxnId
Property.Documentation: This is the portion of the paycheck that is to be direct deposit into the employee account.

DataEntity.Name: PayrollRun
DataEntity.Documentation: This entity will identify the origination point of a transaction. The majority will be origination from source sytems, but wire transfers, reversals, fee transactions from MME and responses from ACH will all affect the ledger and generate transactions.

Property.Name: PayrollDirectDepositAmount
Property.Documentation: Total dd amount of the entire payroll run.

DataEntity.Name: SourcePayrollParameter
DataEntity.Documentation: Contains parameters for various settings for each source system. Because a parameter setting can change over time, the parameter history will be preserved through effective dating.

DataEntity.Name: TransactionOffloadBatch
DataEntity.Documentation: This Entity will store Txn associated with a Offload Batch. This relation is 1-->M but changed to 1-->M by an association table, so that Offload Batch Gseq is not part of the core table Financial Txn.

Property.Name: OffloadStatusCd
Property.Documentation: Offload status for an individual txn, valid values NULL, PENDING,ERROR,EXECUTED

DataEntity.Name: TransactionResponse
DataEntity.Documentation: A persistent package of transactional data that has been (or will be) supplied to the source payroll system. A record of what was returned to the client payroll system in relation to a transactional web service request or server originated transaction. This record can be used to recreate the package should the client need it at a later date.

DataEntity.Name: TransactionReturn
DataEntity.Documentation: This table contains a relationship between transactions and when they were returned. If a return cannot be associated with a txn, then the txn will be null.

Property.Name: BankReturnCd
Property.Documentation: We won't validate this field from lookup table as its non-updated field owned by MME. Ach Returns handler will populate this along with Desc. The only reason we need to store this is because QBOE has some workflow associated with return CD e.g. if acct type is invalid, qboe will automatically re-submit txn with other acct type.

Property.Name: BankReturnTraceNumber
Property.Documentation: This field will map back to ACH Trace Nbr.

Property.Name: ReturnStatusCd
Property.Documentation: Initially set by return handler but can be overridden by rep based upon workflow step. valid values: CREATED - when originally inserted ERROR if integrity Exceptions occur OPEN RESOLVED WRITTEN OFF Field is bases on Rep UI requirement. The only 3 know status are OPEN, IN PROGRESS and CLOSED.

DataEntity.Name: TransactionReturnBatch
DataEntity.Documentation: This table contains a return of transactions from the MME component. It will not contain a reference back to it's offload batch. Only errors are returned. If all is well with the offload, there will be no return batch.

Property.Name: ACHReturnFileName
Property.Documentation: This field will be populated by the file id of respose file received from bank.

Property.Name: StatusCd
Property.Documentation: Status of a offload Batch. Not Null field default value would be PENDING when its created. Can have 3 Values PENDING COMPLETED ERROR

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

DataEntity.Name: TaxCredits9061
DataEntity.Documentation: Stores 9061 forms for Tax Credits

Property.Name: RecordMetadata
Property.Documentation: This column stores the record metadata in a json format. More details https://wiki.intuit.com/x/7xhJLw

Property.Name: Id
Property.Documentation: GUID for primary key because no single column is completely unique.

DataObject.Name: PayrollFrequency
DataObject.Documentation: This table contains the number of payrolls in a given year. Could apply to both companies and employees.

DataObject.Name: BankHoliday
DataObject.Documentation: This table contains the days the US banks are closed in order to recognize days off at the national level. This table gets its values from the Federal Reserve.

DataObject.Name: EventType
DataObject.Documentation: Will store company event type. For DD there are currently 3 EVENT that we would capture 1. DD Failure 2. Strikes 3. DD Limits

DataObject.Name: LedgerAccount
DataObject.Documentation: The chart of accounts that make up the ledger.

DataObject.Name: Service
DataObject.Documentation: A look up table that contains all Intuit OSP payroll products and/or services offered.

DataObject.Name: SourceSystem
DataObject.Documentation: Systems who subscribe to the PSE-DD Service. For now mostly it would be payroll Services.

DataObject.Name: TransactionState
DataObject.Documentation: The status of a transaction at a given point in time. The transaction transitions from start to completion or error. Think of this as the set of events for a state machine that happen to transactions.

DataObject.Name: TransactionType
DataObject.Documentation: The type of a financial transaction. Typically either a debit or credit from an employer or employee.

Field.Name: SignupEvent
Field.Documentation: The offer begins at the moment the customer signs up for the payroll service (offering)

Field.Name: ActivationEvent
Field.Documentation: The offer begins at the moment the customer activates the payroll service (offering) (when this is different from signing up)

Field.Name: FirstUseEvent
Field.Documentation: The offer begins when the customer submits his first payroll

Field.Name: RedemptionEvent
Field.Documentation: The offer begins when the customer claims (redeems) the offer -- or when an agent does so for him

Field.Name: DateEvent
Field.Documentation: The offer ends on a specific date

Field.Name: DurationEvent
Field.Documentation: The offer ends a certain number of days after it began

Field.Name: PayrollUsageEvent
Field.Documentation: The offer ends after a certain number of payroll submissions (after it began)
